title: ilex crenata var. radicans
intro: Ilex crenata var. radicans, also known as Japanese Holly or Box-Leaf Holly, is a compact, evergreen shrub native to Japan. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Ilex crenata var. radicans include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. Ilex crenata var. radicans can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
